SpruceX LLM.txt
Version: 1.0.0
Status: Active development, not complete yet.

Overview

SpruceX is a lightweight reactive HTML framework that combines Alpine-style
declarative state with HTMX-style network actions. It uses `sx-*` attributes on
plain HTML and auto-initializes on `DOMContentLoaded`.

Install / Entry Points

- Browser bundle: `/dist/sprucex.js` or `/dist/sprucex.min.js`
- Package entry points: `lib/sprucex.mjs`, `lib/sprucex.cjs`, `lib/sprucex.d.ts`

Quick Start

<script src="/dist/sprucex.js"></script>

<div sx-data="{ count: 0 }">
  <button sx-on:click="count++">+1</button>
  <span sx-text="count"></span>
</div>

Core Directives

- `sx-data`: component root state, object/function/factory-like expression
- `sx-text`: write `textContent`
- `sx-html`: write `innerHTML`
- `sx-show`: toggle display based on truthiness
- `sx-bind:<attr>`: bind arbitrary attributes
- `sx-class`: conditional classes while preserving original classes
- `sx-toggle`: toggle a boolean state key on click
- `sx-model`: two-way binding for inputs/selects/textareas
- `sx-model` modifiers: `.trim`, `.number`, `.lazy`, plus `sx-model.debounce-ms="N"`
- `sx-ref`: register elements on `$refs`
- `sx-for`: loop on `<template>`
- `sx-memo`: cache `sx-text` / `sx-html` based on dependency keys
- `sx-local`: persist component state in `localStorage`
- `sx-lazy`: defer component init until visible
- `sx-error-fallback`: fallback expression if a binding throws
- `script[sx-init-data]`: embed JSON for identifier-style `sx-data` roots

Expressions / Helpers

- `$event`: current DOM event in handlers
- `$refs`: object of `sx-ref` elements
- `$store(name)`: access global stores
- `$emit(name, detail)`: emit component-scoped custom events
- `$index`: default loop index inside `sx-for`

Lifecycle / State Notes

- State is deeply reactive, including nested objects and common array mutators.
- Component hooks supported in `sx-data`: `init()`, `mounted()`, `destroyed()`
- A `watch` object may be defined in component state to react to property changes.
- Global shared state is created with `SpruceX.store(name, value, { persist })`.
- Named data factories can be registered with `SpruceX.data(name, factory)`.

Event Handling

- `sx-on:<event>` attaches handlers
- Supported modifiers: `.prevent`, `.stop`, `.self`, `.window`, `.document`
- Keyboard modifiers use key names such as `.enter` and `.escape`

Network Directives

- Request methods: `sx-get`, `sx-post`, `sx-put`, `sx-delete`
- Triggering: `sx-trigger`, `sx-trigger-debounce`
- Targeting and swaps: `sx-target`, `sx-swap`
- URL/body/header helpers: `sx-vars`, `sx-body`, `sx-body-type`, `sx-headers`, `sx-include`
- Request state targets: `sx-json-into`, `sx-loading-into`, `sx-error-into`
- UX helpers: `sx-disable-while-request`, `sx-text-while-request`, `sx-confirm`
- Request flow: `sx-optimistic`, `sx-revert-on-error`, `sx-poll`, `sx-poll-while`
- Cancellation: `sx-cancel-previous`

Request Notes

- Default trigger is `click`, or `submit` on forms.
- `sx-trigger` also supports inline debounce syntax such as `input.debounce.300`.
- Supported swap modes: `innerHTML`, `outerHTML`, `before`, `after`, `prepend`, `append`, `morph`
- `sx-cancel-previous` aborts only the previous in-flight request for the same binding.
- Aborted cancelable requests do not emit error events, do not write `sx-error-into`,
  and do not run `sx-revert-on-error`.
- Cancelable requests are also aborted during component refresh and destroy.

Navigation / Morphing

- `sx-page`: mark the SPA navigation container
- `sx-boost`: prefetch links on hover/focus
- `sx-boost-on`: customize boost events
- `sx-key`: preserve identity during list diffs and morphs
- `sx-preserve`: skip morphing for a specific element
- `SpruceX.navigate(url)`: navigate via SPA page swapping
- `SpruceX.prefetch(url)`: prefetch a page into cache
- `SpruceX.clearCache()`: clear the page cache
- `SpruceX.morph(target, source)`: morph DOM content in place

Built-In Integrations

- `sx-animate`: auto-animate list/container children
- `sx-chart`, `sx-chart-type`, `sx-chart-options`: Chart.js integration
- `sx-gridstack`, `sx-gridstack-options`, `sx-gridstack-option:<name>`: GridStack integration
- `sx-gridstack-on-change`, `sx-gridstack-on-added`, `sx-gridstack-on-removed`,
  `sx-gridstack-on-dragstop`, `sx-gridstack-on-resizestop`: write GridStack events into state

Animation / Boot Notes

- `sx-animate` requires the Auto Animate library to be loaded separately unless
  you wire it through `SpruceX.setAutoAnimate(...)`.
- `window.SpruceXBoot.initTheme()` runs once before component auto-init.
- `SpruceX.animate(el, options)` manually enables auto-animate on a DOM element.

JavaScript API

- `SpruceX.init()`
- `SpruceX.store(name, value, { persist })`
- `SpruceX.data(name, factory)`
- `SpruceX.inspect()`
- `SpruceX.navigate(url)`
- `SpruceX.prefetch(url)`
- `SpruceX.clearCache()`
- `SpruceX.morph(target, source)`
- `SpruceX.animate(el, options)`
- `SpruceX.setAutoAnimate(autoAnimate)`
- `SpruceX.integration(name, integration)`

Plugin Authoring

SpruceX plugins are integrations registered with:
`SpruceX.integration(name, integration)`

Hooks

1) `setup(component)` optional one-time setup per component instance
2) `scan(component, el)` optional element scan hook used to collect bindings
3) `update(component)` optional render/update hook
4) `teardown(component)` optional cleanup hook

Useful component helpers

- `component.evaluateExpressionOrLiteral(raw)`
- `component.assignStateValue(targetExpr, value)`
- `component.state`
- `component.root`

Minimal plugin example

<script>
  SpruceX.integration("uppercase", {
    setup(component) {
      component._uppercaseBindings = [];
    },
    scan(component, el) {
      const expr = el.getAttribute("sx-uppercase");
      if (!expr) return;
      component._uppercaseBindings.push({ el, expr });
    },
    update(component) {
      (component._uppercaseBindings || []).forEach(({ el, expr }) => {
        const value = component.evaluateExpressionOrLiteral(expr);
        el.textContent = String(value ?? "").toUpperCase();
      });
    },
    teardown(component) {
      component._uppercaseBindings = [];
    },
  });
</script>

<div sx-data="{ name: 'sprucex' }">
  <span sx-uppercase="name"></span>
</div>
